qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: XSS vulnerability in net_drawer via unsanitized doc_string injection
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes path traversal via symlink in onnx/checker.cc:1024-1033 where is_regular_file follows symlinks. This finding is about XSS via insufficient sanitization of doc_string in onnx/tools/net_drawer.py:45-67, which is a different vulnerability class (XSS vs path traversal), different component (net_drawer vs checker), and different root cause (missing output sanitization vs ineffective symlink detection).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes a path traversal via symlink in onnx/checker.cc:1024-1033 where std::filesystem::is_regular_file follows symbolic links, allowing arbitrary file read outside the model directory. The only qualifying finding is XSS in net_drawer.py via unsanitized doc_string, which is a different vulnerability class (XSS vs path traversal), different component (net_drawer vs checker), and different root cause (missing output sanitization vs ineffective symlink detection).
evidence: Advisory vulnerable code: onnx/checker.cc:1024-1033 (symlink check via is_regular_file)
Advisory CWE: CWE-23 (Relative Path Traversal), CWE-61 (UNIX Symbolic Link Following)
xss_in_net_drawer targets onnx/tools/net_drawer.py:45-67 (different component, different vulnerability class)
